title: Fix dodge mechanic to use Pokemon repositioning instead of button press

requirements.summary: Replace dodge button with drag-to-reposition mechanic for Pokemon dodging in real-time battle
acceptanceCriteria: No 'Dodge' button or dodge-keybind is present in the battle UI.
The player's Pokemon sprite is draggable (touch drag on mobile, click-drag on desktop) to any position within the player's side of the battle field.
When the player drags their Pokemon away from the path of an incoming opponent attack projectile, the attack misses and deals no damage.
When the player does NOT reposition their Pokemon out of the path of an incoming attack, the attack connects and deals damage normally.
Repositioning feels responsive and immediate — the Pokemon sprite follows the drag in real time with no noticeable lag.
The dodge detection uses positional overlap between the incoming attack projectile and the Pokemon sprite's current position to determine hit or miss.
The mechanic works consistently on both mobile (touch) and desktop (mouse) inputs.

file_operations.description: Remove the dodge button UI element and its associated click handler. Add state tracking for player Pokemon position (x, y coordinates). Implement collision detection logic that compares incoming attack projectile position with player Pokemon position during attack animations. Update the damage calculation to skip damage application when no collision is detected. Ensure the real-time, non-turn-based battle flow is preserved.

Add drag interaction handlers for both mouse (onMouseDown, onMouseMove, onMouseUp) and touch (onTouchStart, onTouchMove, onTouchEnd) events to the player's Pokemon sprite. Implement position state that updates in real-time as the user drags. Apply CSS transform to reposition the sprite based on drag coordinates. Ensure the sprite remains within the player's side of the battle field (boundary constraints). Pass the current position up to the parent Battle component for collision detection. Maintain existing animation classes (idle, attack, hit, faint) and ensure they work with repositioning.

Update the projectile animation to accept the current player Pokemon position as a prop. Modify the projectile trajectory calculation to use the actual Pokemon position instead of a fixed target. Implement collision detection by checking overlap between the projectile bounding box and the player Pokemon sprite bounding box during the 'launch' and 'impact' phases. Return collision status (hit or miss) to the parent Battle component. Ensure the three-phase animation (launch, impact, done) still plays correctly, with the impact phase only triggering visually if a collision occurred.
